channel.title: teamsiems
channel.description: A team of Siems making observations on the web.

item.title: Happy 21st Blogiversary
item.dc:creator: chris
item.content:encoded: I began teamsiems zeitgeist on 2/16/2005 with a simple post, teamsiems zeitgeist defined.

item.title: Project Anchor
item.dc:creator: chris
item.content:encoded:
Eschatology: a branch of theology concerned with the final events in the history of the world or of humankind.

Source: merriam-webster.com

Project Anchor is described as a NASA initiative focused on controlling gravitational waves. According to the story, two black holes collided billions of years ago, sending gravitational waves toward Earth. As these waves traveled, they supposedly passed through dark matter, which “lensed” or amplified them. Scientists are said to predict that the waves will reach Earth on August 12, 2026, at 14:33 UTC (9:33 AM CDT), triggering a “gravitational discontinuity event” during which Earth would temporarily lose gravity for seven seconds.

This raises the question: Is Project Anchor just another end‑of‑the‑world prediction like the Mayan calendar scare in 2012 or the Y2K panic in 2000, or is there any truth behind it?

Busted

It's a viral TikTok hoax. On February 12 (6 months before the "event"), I asked Bing Chat (AI) to summarize an the news about Project Anchor. This is what it returned:

Summary of the topic (based on established facts)

1. “NASA Project Anchor” is not real

There is no NASA program called Project Anchor.
The name appears only in viral TikTok videos and social‑media posts, not in any NASA documentation, budget filings, or scientific publications.

2. The “gravity blackout” claim is a hoax

The viral claim suggests:

  • Earth will “lose gravity” for a short period
  • NASA warned people to prepare
  • It will cause people to float or collapse

None of this is scientifically possible.

Gravity is caused by Earth’s mass. For Earth to “lose gravity,” the planet would need to lose its mass — which cannot happen suddenly or temporarily.

3. NASA has publicly debunked similar hoaxes before

NASA routinely clarifies that:

  • Gravity cannot shut off
  • No NASA alert has ever predicted such an event
  • Viral “science” videos often misuse NASA’s name

4. What would happen if gravity disappeared?

Purely hypothetically:

  • Everything not anchored would fly off the planet
  • The atmosphere would escape into space
  • Oceans would disperse
  • Earth’s orbit and rotation would destabilize

But again — this cannot happen in reality.

5. Why these hoaxes spread

The article you referenced likely discusses:

  • TikTok videos claiming NASA issued warnings
  • Misinterpretations of NASA’s budget or research
  • Public reactions and jokes
  • Clarifications from scientists debunking the claim

ogo-large-2/cropped-teamsiemsLogo-large-2.png?_i=AA&";s:140:"https://res.cloudinary.com/teamsiems/images/f_auto,q_auto/v1585929789/cropped-teamsiemsLogo-large-2/cropped-teamsiemsLogo-large-2.png?_i=AA&";s:35:"https://res.cloudinary.com/teamsiems/images/v1585929789/cropped-teamsiemsLogo-large-2/cropped-teamsiemsLogo-large-2.png?_i=AA";s:139:"https://res.cloudinary.com/teamsiems/images/f_auto,q_auto/v1585929789/cropped-teamsiemsLogo-large-2/cropped-teamsiemsLogo-large-2.png?_i=AA";s:34:"https://res.cloudinary.com/teamsiems/images/v1770741767/ShockingNews_9348104d7/ShockingNews_9348104d7.png?_i=AA";s:125:"https://res.cloudinary.com/teamsiems/images/f_auto,q_auto/v1770741767/ShockingNews_9348104d7/ShockingNews_9348104d7.png?_i=AA";s:29:"https://res.cloudinary.com/teamsiems/images/v1770741767/ShockingNews_9348104d7/ShockingNews_9348104d7.png?_i=AA&";s:126:"https://res.cloudinary.com/teamsiems/images/f_auto,q_auto/v1770741767/ShockingNews_9348104d7/ShockingNews_9348104d7.png?_i=AA&";s:28:"https://res.cloudinary.com/teamsiems/images/v1770741767/ShockingNews_9348104d7/ShockingNews_9348104d7.png?_i=AA";s:125:"https://res.cloudinary.com/teamsiems/images/f_auto,q_auto/v1770741767/ShockingNews_9348104d7/ShockingNews_9348104d7.png?_i=AA";}}

item.title: Operation Epic Fury
item.dc:creator: chris
item.content:encoded:
Operation Epic Fury, the U.S. code name for its joint military campaign with Israel against Iran, officially commenced at 1:15 AM EDT on February 28, 2026. The operation involved coordinated strikes targeting Iranian military and nuclear infrastructure, including command and control centers, missile and drone launch sites, air defense systems, and naval assets. Israel simultaneously launched its own campaign, dubbed Operation Roaring Lion, in coordination with U.S. forces.

Context and Objectives

The operation was launched in response to Iran’s nuclear activities and missile threats, with the stated goal of dismantling the Iranian regime’s security apparatus and preventing the development of nuclear weapons capable of threatening the United States and its allies. The opening strikes targeted high-value locations, including the offices of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and senior IRGC commanders, and triggered retaliatory missile and drone attacks from Iran.

Initial Execution

The first wave of strikes included precision munitions launched from air, land, and sea, and marked the first combat use of low-cost one-way attack drones by CENTCOM's Task Force Scorpion Strike. The operation represented the largest regional concentration of American military firepower in a generation, with over 1,700 U.S. sorties and thousands of munitions deployed in the opening 48 hours.

Summary

In short, Operation Epic Fury started on February 28, 2026, with a massive, coordinated U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran’s strategic and nuclear infrastructure, marking a significant escalation in regional military operations.

item.title: Artemis, Returning to the Moon
item.dc:creator: chris
item.content:encoded:
Artemis, Greek goddess of the Moon, will follow in twin brother Apollo's footsteps, and return people to the Moon.

Beginning with the Artemis Accords in 2020, NASA and 7 other countries established the foundations to explore the Moon, Mars, and beyond. Since 2020 there has been much planning, research, and development. They planned the first 5 missions to hopefully begin building a Moon base.

Artemis I: Successfully completed as an uncrewed test flight of the Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft. It launched from Kennedy Space Center on November 16, 2022, and splashed down December 11, 2022.

Artemis II: Successfully completed first crewed flight to the Moon since 1972! It launched from Kennedy Space Center on April 1, 2026, and splashed down in the Pacific Ocean April 10, 2026. "The first crewed Artemis flight marks a key step toward long‑term return to the Moon and future missions to Mars." - NASA

Artemis III: Set to launch in 2027. NASA's Artemis III mission in low Earth orbit will test integrated operations between the Orion spacecraft and one or both commercial landers from SpaceX and Blue Origin respectively.

Artemis IV: Set to launch in 2028. Humanity's return to the lunar surface.

Artemis V: Set to launch in 2028.
